Fix force-approval spam, conflict warning dedup, TE coverage signal, PE worker status

Bug fixes:
- Force-approval: each reviewer agent now checks if it's actually a required
  reviewer for the PR before posting a force-approval comment. Previously all
  3 agents (PE, PM, Architect) would post redundant force-approvals even when
  only 2 were needed for merge.
- Conflict warnings: check existing PR comments before posting duplicate
  conflict detection warnings. PR #317 had 6 identical warnings from 6 commits.
- TE coverage signal: added CheckTestCoverageCompleteAsync() that sets status
  to 'coverage met, tests complete' when all merged code PRs are tested. The
  HealthMonitor picks this up to emit testing.coverage.met, unblocking the
  Testing→Review phase transition. Previously this signal was NEVER emitted.
- PE worker status: worker PEs now show 'Working on tasks' instead of the
  misleading 'Orchestrating tasks' which is leader-only behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(seo): migrate previousSlugs to locale-aware previousSlugsByLocale

PROBLEM: previousSlugs was a flat array with no locale context. When a
job's FR slug changed but the old slug matched the active DE slug, the
safety nets removed it — causing 404s for /fr/trouver-emploi-tessin/<old-slug>.

SOLUTION: New locale-aware format previousSlugsByLocale: { fr: [...], de: [...] }
with per-locale safety nets that only strip matches within the same locale.

Core changes:
- 6 helper functions in dedicated-crawler-common.mjs:
  addPreviousSlugForLocale, getPreviousSlugsForLocale, getAllPreviousSlugs,
  cleanPreviousSlugsPerLocale, syncLegacyPreviousSlugs, mergePreviousSlugsByLocale
- Rewrote captureLostSlugs() to write per-locale
- Fixed all 4 safety nets (hardenJobLocaleFields, writeJobsCrawlerSlice,
  regenerate-slugs-by-locale, cleanPreviousSlugsPerLocale)
- Fixed all write points (mergePreserveLocaleData, preferJob)
- Fixed build plugin bridge page generation (locale-aware)
- Fixed runtime consumers (JobBoard.tsx, router.ts, localeJobsSplitPlugin)
- Updated scatter-jobs, find-orphan, assemble-jobs-dataset

Data migration:
- Recovered 106 previousSlugs lost in commit 7723f77f1 cleanup
- Migrated 3680 flat previousSlugs to locale-aware format across 111 files
- Legacy flat previousSlugs kept in sync for backward compatibility

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: improve scoring reliability — error isolation, retry limits, name matching (#67)

* fix: improve scoring reliability — error isolation, retry limits, name matching

- Wrap each match scoring in try-catch so one failure doesn't crash entire cron
- Add 10-attempt limit — permanently fail matches after max retries
- Guard against empty performances: fail (not sync) if no batting data extracted
- Guard against all-unmatched: skip league scoring if all player names fail to match
- Store unmatched_names in fantasy_scores.breakdown for debugging
- Log unmatched player names in cron output with match/league context
- Add markFailed() helper for consistent error tracking in cricket_sync_tracker
- Filter out permanently failed matches (>=10 attempts) from loadPendingMatches query

Name matching improvements:
- Add deep normalization: strip dots, hyphens, apostrophes before matching
- Handle short last names (<4 chars): try full-string contains and unique last name match
- Add 2-char first name prefix fallback when initial matching narrows to 0

Dismissal detection:
- Check additional CricAPI field names: mode, how_out, wicketCode, out_desc

Fallback improvements:
- Try Cricsheet cache on ANY CricAPI error (not just rate-limit)
- Covers HTTP 500, timeouts, network errors — only re-throw if both providers fail

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address review — atomic attempt increment, lookup tracker for all matches

- Look up attempt count from cricket_sync_tracker for newly discovered matches
  (not just pendingById) to prevent bypassing the max-attempts check
- Use atomic increment RPC (increment_sync_attempts) to avoid race condition
  when cron runs concurrently with manual triggers
- Add migration 017 for the increment_sync_attempts RPC

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(seo): align sitemap previousSlug paths with bridge page locale paths

The sitemap generator was adding ALL previousSlugs under the Italian
path (/cerca-lavoro-ticino/), but the bridge page generator correctly
places locale-specific previousSlugs under their locale prefix
(e.g. /en/find-jobs-ticino/, /fr/trouver-emploi-tessin/).

This mismatch caused 4 sitemap URLs to reference pages that didn't
exist in dist/, blocking deploy at the validate-sitemap-links step.

Fix: Rewrite the sitemap previousSlug section to mirror the bridge
page generator's locale-aware logic:
- previousSlugsByLocale[locale] entries → locale-specific path
- Legacy flat previousSlugs → Italian path (matching bridge behavior)
- Added deduplication via prevSlugSitemapPaths set

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: normalize warning_pct to 2 decimal places for lossless UI round-trip

The frontend form uses integers 0-100 but the backend stores 0-1 decimal.
Values with excess precision (e.g., 0.3333) would lose data on edit via
Math.round(). Adding a Pydantic field_validator with ROUND_HALF_UP on both

to a whole percentage.

periods (months with zero expenses are excluded from the average).

fix: replace DEFAULT_VARIANT_SENTINEL with asset-name template matching

The sentinel approach was broken for packages like uv and copilot-cli
where extract_variant_from_asset returns non-None values (e.g. '64', 'apple',
'gh-copilot') for default binaries due to imperfect platform pattern stripping.

Replace with normalize_asset_for_matching() which strips version numbers
and compares normalized asset names directly, bypassing extract_variant_from_asset
entirely for the no-named-variant update case.

Fixes: 'Variant (default) no longer available' error for uv, copilot-cli,
ripgrep, and any other package with unusual asset naming.

locale: translate missing terms for 15 locales, update english-ok allowlist (#8587)

Translates missing ChurchCRM UI terms across 15 locales (1,941 terms)
and updates `english-ok.json` so the POEditor uploader accepts value=key
entries that are intentionally English (brand names, cognates, locale
names).

## Changes

### Small/medium locales translated inline
- **zh-CN** (5), **zh-TW** (6), **pt-br** (4), **pt** (6), **es / es-AR
/ es-CO / es-MX / es-SV** (7 each), **tr** (6), **fr** (20)
- Terms like `BCC`, `N/A`, `name@example.com` left as `""` per
convention
- `Azure`, `Manual`, `Material`, `Kiosk`, `Malayalam - India`, `Filipino
- Philippines`, colour names etc. translated as value=key (intentionally
English)

### Large locales translated via sub-agents
- **TIER-1:** `hi` (43), `ar` (190), `de` (198), `id` (196), `ja` (190),
`ko` (190), `ru` (189), `vi` (191)
- Denomination-appropriate vocabulary applied per locale (e.g. Coptic
for ar, Lutheran for de, Orthodox for ru)
- Plural forms (`zero/one/two/few/many/other`) filled for Arabic and
Russian

### `english-ok.json` updated
Added entries for all newly translated locales so
`poeditor-upload-missing.js` doesn't skip them as "suspect"
identical-to-source terms:
```json
{
  "tr": ["Azure", "Kiosk", "4. Kiosk"],
  "fr": ["Azure", "Confession", "Format", "Minimum", "Options", ...],
  "es":  ["Azure", "Manual", "Material", "Malayalam - India"],
  ...
}
```

## Remaining (~22 locales, ~3,850 terms)
`af`, `sq`, `am`, `cs`, `nl`, `et`, `fil`, `fi`, `el`, `he`, `hu`, `it`,
`ml`, `nb`, `pl`, `ro`, `sv`, `sw`, `ta`, `te`, `th`, `uk` — not yet
translated. `af` and `sq` translation JSON was produced but not applied
before session ended.

fix: use req.Host for X-Forwarded-Host header (#5)

because Go's net/http promotes the Host header to req.Host and
removes it from the header map. Read req.Host directly, before it
is overwritten with the upstream URL host.

commit.message:
Enable surrogate pair range tests in character classes (#149)

* Add \\uhhhh unicode escape support to parser

Parse \\uhhhh (exactly 4 hex digits) in regex patterns, matching JDK
behavior. When a high surrogate is followed by a \\uhhhh low surrogate,
they are combined into a single supplementary code point.

Fixes #133

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add inline flags (?d) and (?u); fix (?U) to mean UNICODE_CHARACTER_CLASS

Add (?d) for UNIX_LINES and (?u) for UNICODE_CASE inline flags.
Remap (?U) from RE2's NON_GREEDY to JDK's UNICODE_CHARACTER_CLASS,
which enables both UNICODE_GROUPS and UNICODE_CHAR_CLASS.

Fixes #134

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix nested repetition limit for alternation branches

countRepeat was compounding repeat counts across alternation branches
sequentially, treating them as if all branches were visited. For
ALTERNATE nodes, only one branch is taken at match time, so only the
worst-case (most expensive) branch should be considered.

Fixes #135

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make Unicode property name lookup loose per UTS#18

Property name lookups now ignore case, underscores, hyphens, and spaces,
matching JDK behavior and UTS#18 requirements. Both binary properties
and script/category names use the same normalization. For example,
\p{IsWhiteSpace} now matches \p{IsWhite_Space}.

Fixes #136

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Accept \b{g} grapheme cluster boundary syntax

Parse \b{g} without error for JDK compatibility. Currently approximated
as an empty match (matches at every position) since full UAX#29 boundary
detection is not yet implemented.

Fixes #137

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix three-digit octal escape \0nnn parsing

The \0 escape now consumes up to three octal digits (max value 0377),
matching JDK behavior. Previously only two digits were consumed, so
\0101 was parsed as \010 + literal '1' instead of octal 101 (= 'A').

Fixes #138

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implement character class intersection/subtraction (&&)

Add support for the && operator inside character classes, matching JDK
behavior. [a-z&&[def]] now correctly matches only d/e/f, and
[a-z&&[^bc]] correctly excludes b and c.

Added CharClassBuilder.intersect() for computing the intersection of
two character classes using a merge of sorted range sets.

Fixes #139

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Enable surrogate pair range tests in character classes

The \uhhhh surrogate pair combination added in #133 already handles
surrogate pair ranges in character classes correctly. The \u escape
parser combines high+low surrogate pairs into supplementary code points,
so [\uD800\uDC00-\uDBFF\uDFFF] is correctly parsed as [U+10000-U+10FFFF].

Fixes #140

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
